title: Car management system with price prediction
description: Developed a car management platform enabling users to buy, sell, and manage cars with a price-prediction model.

detail.title: Car management system with price prediction
detail.intro: A comprehensive car management system that allows users to buy, sell, and manage their cars efficiently. The platform includes a price-prediction model that helps users determine the optimal price for their vehicles based on factors such as make, model, year, mileage, and market trends.
detail.purpose: Designed to streamline the car listing and selling process, offering a predictive pricing feature based on historical data. The aim was to let users confidently price their cars or evaluate potential purchases. The challenge was integrating a machine learning model seamlessly into a web application while ensuring the system was scalable and user-friendly. A reliable backend manages user data, car listings, and the ML model. As the system handles a growing amount of car data, scalability and performance were key concerns throughout development.

title: Resilient Failure Prediction System for Autonomous Trains
description: Master's thesis: ML system to predict failures in autonomous train systems, achieving 99.95% precision on a 15M-instance public benchmark.

detail.title: Proactive Failure Prediction in Train Air Production Units
detail.intro: Master's thesis at the University of Bejaia in collaboration with LIGM, ESIEE Paris. The project tackles predictive maintenance in autonomous railway systems — predicting failures in air production units before they happen, using gradient-boosted trees on a large public sensor benchmark.
detail.purpose: Unplanned downtime in autonomous trains is expensive and unsafe. The goal was a model that could see failures coming days in advance and explain *why*, so maintenance teams could act with confidence instead of reacting to alarms. I trained an XGBoost model on the MetroPT-3 dataset (15M+ sensor instances from Metro Porto), then layered SHAP explainability on top to surface failure precursors up to three days before incidents. The system outperformed LSTM, Random Forest, and Sparse AutoEncoder baselines, reaching 99.95% precision. The work was accepted for publication.

publication.title: Proactive Failure Prediction in Train Air Production Units Using XGBoost
publication.authors: L. Yahiaoui, A. Aggoune, K. Amrani, M. Mohammedi, K. Hamouid
publication.venue: University of Bejaia & LIGM, ESIEE Paris

title: Blog website

title: Chess game
description: A fully interactive, web-based chess game using modern web technologies. Showcases proficiency in front-end development, state management, and interactive UI design.
